the function y = e^x - x is continuous and differentiable on all real numbers the derivative of the function is y' = e^x - 1 the critical points occur where y' = 0 e^x - 1 = 0 e^x = 1 x = 0 the second derivative of the function is y'' = e^x the second derivative at x = 0 is y'' = 1 the second derivative is positive at x = 0 indicating a local minimum the function has a minimum at x = 0 the minimum value of the function is y = e^0 - 0 = 1 - 0 = 1 the minimum value of the function is 1
